question: Implement a `Time` class with private attributes `hours`, `minutes`, and `seconds` (all integers). Overload the `==` operator to compare two `Time` objects for equality (i.e., they represent the exact same time).
driver:
Time t1(10, 30, 45); Time t2(10, 30, 45); Time t3(10, 30, 46); Time t4(10, 30, 47); Time t5(5, 5, 0); Time t6(5, 5, 1); Time t7(0, 0, 0); Time t8(0, 0, 0); Time t9(1, 2, 3); Time t10(1, 2, 4); Time t11(1, 2, 5); std::cout << (t1 == t2 ? "Equal" : "Not Equal") << "
"; std::cout << (t1 == t3 ? "Equal" : "Not Equal") << "
"; std::cout << (t1 == t4 ? "Equal" : "Not Equal") << "
"; std::cout << (t5 == t6 ? "Equal" : "Not Equal") << "
"; std::cout << (t7 == t8 ? "Equal" : "Not Equal") << "
"; std::cout << (t9 == t10 ? "Equal" : "Not Equal") << "
"; std::cout << (t9 == t11 ? "Equal" : "Not Equal") << "
";
errors: The `operator==` implementation has a robustness issue. It incorrectly considers two `Time` objects equal if their `hours` and `minutes` match, and their `seconds` differ by 0 or 1 (i.e., `std::abs(seconds - other.seconds) <= 1`). The problem statement requires 'exact same time', meaning all three attributes must be identical. This leads to incorrect comparisons for times that are very close but not precisely the same.
